evals.prompt: I want to wrap Stripe's API as a Claude Code connector. Just read-only — list customers, get a customer by ID, list charges. The API takes a Bearer token via STRIPE_API_KEY. Base URL https://api.stripe.com.
evals.expected_output: A working stripe-agent-connector scaffold at /Users/narayan/src/connectors/stripe-agent-connector/. All 19 canonical files present (package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts, README.md, LICENSE, .npmignore, .gitignore, src/cli.ts, src/index.ts, src/lib/stripe_client.ts, src/lib/stripe_error.ts, tests/unit/cli.test.ts, tests/unit/stripe_client_extras.test.ts, tests/integration/framework.test.ts, plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json, plugin/hooks/hooks.json, plugin/hooks/reminder.mjs, plugin/bin/stripe-agent (executable), plugin/skills/stripe-agent/SKILL.md, plugin/commands/stripe-agent.md). Bearer auth in client. Three read-classified actions. npm run build + typecheck + test all green. Smoke envelope is valid JSON with status:error and error_code:CONFIG_ERROR.
expectations: Directory exists at /Users/narayan/src/connectors/stripe-agent-connector/
package.json declares name '@narai/stripe-agent-connector'
package.json depends on '@narai/connector-toolkit' at '^3.1.0', '@narai/credential-providers' at '^0.2.1', '@narai/connector-config' at '^1.1.0', and 'zod' at '^3.23.0'
package.json exposes a bin entry 'stripe-agent-connector' pointing at './dist/cli.js'
src/cli.ts imports loadConnectorEnvironment from '@narai/connector-config' and declares a STRIPE_ENV_MAPPING with token mapped to 'STRIPE_API_KEY'
src/lib/stripe_client.ts has a class StripeClient with a Result-envelope contract (returns StripeResult<T> from request methods)
src/lib/stripe_client.ts builds the Authorization header as `Bearer ${this._token}` (not X-API-Key, not Basic)
src/index.ts uses createConnector from '@narai/connector-toolkit' and registers exactly three actions: get_customer, list_customers, list_charges
All three actions in src/index.ts carry classify: { kind: 'read' }
src/index.ts exports a default connector and re-exports main, fetch, validActions

plugin/hooks/hooks.json declares all four canonical hook arrays: SessionStart (with three commands: npm install diff/install, reminder.mjs, stale-summarize.mjs), PostToolUse (with usage-record.mjs), and SessionEnd (with session-summary.mjs)
plugin/bin/stripe-agent is a bash shim that execs '${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/node_modules/@narai/stripe-agent-connector/dist/cli.js' and is marked executable
plugin/skills/stripe-agent/SKILL.md has frontmatter with name 'stripe-agent' and context 'fork', plus an actions table covering the three Stripe actions
.npmignore excludes src/, tests/, plugin/, evals/
Running `npm run build` in the new directory exits 0 and produces dist/cli.js plus dist/index.js
Running `npm run typecheck` in the new directory exits 0 silently
Running `npm test` in the new directory passes all tests (no failures)
Running `node dist/cli.js --action list_customers --params '{}'` (without STRIPE_API_KEY set) emits a JSON object on stdout with status='error' and error_code='CONFIG_ERROR'

evals.prompt: We have an internal REST API at https://api.acme.internal exposing GET /v1/orders/:id and GET /v1/orders?customer=X. Auth is X-API-Key header set from ACME_API_KEY. Two actions: get_order and list_orders. Pagination uses limit + offset query params, default limit 25, max 100.
evals.expected_output: An acme-agent-connector scaffold using X-API-Key (not Bearer) auth, custom base URL https://api.acme.internal, two read-classified actions, and standard verification passing.
expectations: Directory exists at /Users/narayan/src/connectors/acme-agent-connector/
src/cli.ts maps the auth env var name to ACME_API_KEY
src/lib/acme_client.ts builds the auth header as 'X-API-Key: ${this._token}' (NOT Authorization: Bearer)
src/lib/acme_client.ts uses base URL constant 'https://api.acme.internal'
src/index.ts registers exactly two actions: get_order (params include id) and list_orders (params include limit with default 25 and max 100, plus optional customer)
Both actions classified as read
list_orders Zod schema uses .max(100) on the limit field
Running `npm run build` exits 0
Running `npm test` passes all tests
Smoke envelope from `node dist/cli.js --action get_order --params '{"id":"x"}'` (no creds) returns valid JSON with error_code CONFIG_ERROR

evals.prompt: We have a GraphQL endpoint at https://api.linear.app/graphql. I want to expose two read-only queries: get_issue (takes issue id, returns id, title, state) and search_issues (takes query string and optional limit, returns matching issues). Auth is Bearer LINEAR_API_KEY.
evals.expected_output: A linear-agent-connector scaffold whose client methods do POST /graphql with a query string + variables, two read-classified actions matching the GraphQL queries, and standard verification passing.
expectations: Directory exists at /Users/narayan/src/connectors/linear-agent-connector/
src/lib/linear_client.ts contains methods that POST to '/graphql' (the single GraphQL endpoint pattern)
Each public client method constructs a GraphQL query string with variables and posts it as JSON
src/index.ts registers exactly two actions: get_issue and search_issues, both classified as read
src/cli.ts maps the auth env var to LINEAR_API_KEY
Authorization header is 'Bearer ${this._token}'
Running `npm run build` exits 0 and produces dist/
Running `npm test` passes all tests

evals.prompt: I want to wrap our internal Slack-like messaging API. Two actions: list_channels (read) and post_message (sends a message — definitely a write). API at https://api.acme-msg.internal, auth Bearer ACME_MSG_TOKEN. The message body should support text + optional thread_ts.
evals.expected_output: An acme-msg-agent-connector scaffold where post_message is correctly classified as write, the skill explained the approval-mode implications during interview, and the post-scaffold next-steps mention that the policy gate engages on post_message at runtime.
expectations: Directory exists at /Users/narayan/src/connectors/acme-msg-agent-connector/
src/index.ts registers list_channels with classify: { kind: 'read' }
src/index.ts registers post_message with classify: { kind: 'write' } (NOT read)
post_message Zod schema requires text (string) and accepts optional thread_ts
src/lib/acme-msg_client.ts (or acme_msg_client.ts depending on slug-handling) has a postMessage public method using HTTP POST
During the conversation, the skill explicitly explains approval-mode implications for the write action (transcript should mention 'confirm_once' or 'approval mode' or the toolkit's policy gate)
The skill's post-scaffold message tells the user that post_message will engage the policy gate at runtime
Running `npm run build` exits 0
Running `npm test` passes all tests

evals.prompt: I want to query my company's Postgres database from Claude — specifically a few tables in the orders schema for analytics work.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that db-agent-connector already supports Postgres and redirects the user to install/configure it instead of scaffolding a new connector. No new connector files are created. The response walks through the canonical db-agent setup: which CLAUDE.md to read, how ~/.connectors/db-agent/config.yaml is structured, what policy rules to set, and a smoke command.
expectations: No new directory is created under /Users/narayan/src/connectors/ (no postgres-agent-connector/, no orders-agent-connector/)
The skill's response mentions db-agent-connector by name
The skill's response references /Users/narayan/src/connectors/db-agent-connector/CLAUDE.md as authoritative documentation
The skill's response mentions ~/.connectors/db-agent/config.yaml as the configuration location
The skill's response explains the policy rules (read/dml/ddl/privilege) at least in summary
The skill's response provides a smoke-test command using db-agent-connector
The skill explains why db-agent is the right choice (multi-backend, present_only state, policy-gated) rather than just deflecting
The skill does NOT scaffold any new connector files

evals.prompt: I want to wrap an existing MCP server my team built (it's a wrapper around our incident-response tooling) so other engineers can use it via Claude.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that MCP servers are a different abstraction in Claude Code and points the user at the MCP server config docs. No new connector is scaffolded.
expectations: No new directory is created under /Users/narayan/src/connectors/
The skill's response mentions MCP servers as a different abstraction from connectors
The skill's response points the user at Claude Code's MCP server config (e.g., .mcp.json or the MCP docs)
The skill explicitly does NOT scaffold a new connector
The skill's response gives the user a clear next step (configure MCP server in Claude Code settings, not build a connector)

evals.prompt: I need a connector for our private GitHub Enterprise instance at https://github.acme-corp.internal. Read-only: list_repos for an org, get_repo by name, list_pull_requests for a repo. Auth needs both a personal access token (env: ACME_GH_TOKEN) and a default org name (env: ACME_GH_ORG) — the org gets used in URL paths so it has to be threaded through the client.
evals.expected_output: An acme-gh-agent-connector scaffold using multi-secret auth (token + org), with the org plumbed through the client constructor and used in URL construction for actions that need it.
expectations: Directory exists at /Users/narayan/src/connectors/acme-gh-agent-connector/
src/cli.ts declares an ENV_MAPPING with two entries (token → ACME_GH_TOKEN, org → ACME_GH_ORG)
src/lib/acme-gh_client.ts (or equivalent) has a ClientOptions interface with both token and org as required fields
The Client constructor stores both as private fields (private readonly _token AND private readonly _org)
loadCredentials returns null when either env var is missing
At least one client method uses this._org in the URL path (e.g., for list_repos)
Three actions registered: list_repos, get_repo, list_pull_requests (all classified as read)
Running `npm run build` exits 0
Running `npm test` passes all tests

evals.prompt: Wrap Salesforce REST API as a connector. Auth is OAuth 2.0 with refresh tokens — we have a client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token, exchanged at /services/oauth2/token for an access token. I want list_accounts and get_account actions to start.
evals.expected_output: A salesforce-agent-connector scaffold that explicitly flags OAuth as not-templated, leaves a placeholder loadSalesforceCredentials with a TODO for the OAuth flow, and the skill's post-scaffold next-steps tell the user they have to implement the refresh-token exchange before the connector will work against the live API.
expectations: Directory exists at /Users/narayan/src/connectors/salesforce-agent-connector/
src/lib/salesforce_client.ts has a loadSalesforceCredentials function with a TODO comment indicating OAuth flow needs implementation
The TODO comment mentions: reading a refresh token, exchanging at the token endpoint, caching the access token until expiry
The skill's response to the user includes an explicit warning that OAuth is not templated and requires manual implementation
Two actions registered: list_accounts and get_account, both classified as read
src/lib/salesforce_client.ts builds requests with an Authorization header of the form 'Bearer ${this._access_token}' (using a placeholder access_token field that the OAuth flow would populate)
The post-scaffold next-steps explicitly tell the user the OAuth flow must be filled in before live use

evals.prompt: I have a CLI tool called 'incident-cli' installed system-wide. It supports `incident-cli list --service=<name>` and `incident-cli show <id>`. I want a connector that wraps these as actions called list_incidents and get_incident.
evals.expected_output: An incident-cli-agent-connector scaffold whose client methods shell out to the incident-cli binary via child_process (instead of HTTP fetch), with two read-classified actions.
expectations: Directory exists at /Users/narayan/src/connectors/incident-cli-agent-connector/ (or similar slug)
src/lib/<slug>_client.ts uses node:child_process (execFile or spawn) instead of HTTP fetch for at least the action methods
list_incidents method invokes 'incident-cli list --service=<name>' style command
get_incident method invokes 'incident-cli show <id>' style command
Both actions classified as read
stdout from the CLI is parsed into the Result envelope shape
Errors from the CLI (non-zero exit, stderr) are translated into the error envelope with retriable: false
Running `npm run build` exits 0

evals.prompt: I'm working on the existing notion-agent-connector and want to add a new action called search_blocks that takes a block_id and returns the block's children. Can you help?
evals.expected_output: The skill correctly identifies this as a modification to an EXISTING connector, not a new scaffolding job. The skill declines to scaffold and points the user at the existing notion-agent-connector source files (specifically src/index.ts and src/lib/notion_client.ts) where the new action should be added.
expectations: No new directory is created (no search-blocks-agent-connector/, no notion-blocks-agent-connector/, etc.)
The skill's response makes clear this is a modification to the existing connector, not a new scaffold
The skill points the user at the actual files to edit: notion-agent-connector/src/index.ts (for the action definition) and notion-agent-connector/src/lib/notion_client.ts (for the client method)
The skill does NOT walk through the standard interview (slug, auth, base URL, etc.) since none of that applies
The skill does NOT invoke the create-connector scaffolding path
